data.title: U.S. puts new restrictions on China's access to AI chips, tools
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Commerce has placed additional curbs on China’s access to vital tools and components for AI chips.

The new rules restrict sales of 27 types of high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and chipmaking equipment, including products made outside the country by American companies, and are in addition to existing curbs affecting advanced logic chips.

The department has also blacklisted an additional 140 Chinese entities that it said have been operating on behalf of the Chinese government. The names of these companies were not given in the initial statement.

The full list of new sanctions and company additions will be detailed later on Monday, a U.S. official told Bloomberg last week.

“This action is the culmination of the Biden-Harris Administration’s targeted approach, in concert with our allies and partners, to impair the PRC’s ability to indigenize the production of advanced technologies that pose a risk to our national security,” Secretary of Commerce Gina Raimondo said in a statement. “No Administration has been tougher in strategically addressing China’s military modernization through export controls.”

The controls unveiled Monday include exemptions for ally countries such as the Netherlands and Japan, according to a senior administration official, which are expected to impose similar restrictions. Neither government has so far issued such additional rules.

South Korea’s SK Hynix (OTCPK:HXSCF), a key Nvidia (NVDA) supplier, is a current leader in providing HBM chips, followed by Micron Technology (MU) -0.77% and Samsung Electronics (OTCPK:SSNLF). The controls will may also likely hurt U.S. chipmakers like Lam Research (LRCX) -0.55%, KLA Corp (KLAC) and Applied Materials (AMAT) -0.32%.

data.title: ReGen III appoints Tony Weatherill as CEO and President
data.description:
• ReGen III (OTCQB:ISRJF) announces the appointment of Tony Weatherill as CEO and President, effective December 2, 2024.
  • Weatherill most recently served as Senior Vice President, Commercial, at H.F. Sinclair (DINO). Concurrently, Tony also served as president of Petro-Canada Lubricants (a division of H.F. Sinclair).
  • With Weatherill's appointment, Mark Redcliffe, current CEO and President of ReGen III, will transition to the newly created roles of Executive Vice President and Chief Strategy Officer.

data.title: Global arms sales rise on demand linked to Ukraine and Gaza wars, Asia tensions
data.description:
Arms sales of the top 100 defense companies grew last year, driven by demand linked to the wars in Gaza and Ukraine as well as growing East Asia tensions, according to think tank Stockholm International Peace Research Institute.

Sales of arms and military services by the 100 companies reached $632B in 2023, up 4.2% from a year ago, bouncing back after a dip in 2022. Higher arms revenue was seen in all regions, with particularly sharp increases in companies based in Russia and the Middle East.

The trend "is likely to continue in 2024," said Lorenzo Scarazzato, researcher, SIPRI Military Expenditure and Arms Production Program. He added that the data "did not fully reflect the scale of demand" and many companies appear to be "optimistic about future sales."

Forty-one U.S. companies in the top 100 defense firms recorded arms revenues of $317B in 2023, half the top 100's overall revenues and 2.5% higher than a year ago.

Notably, the world's largest arms producers Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) and RTX (NYSE:RTX) registered a drop in sales due to supply chain challenges in 2023.

Over in Europe, arms revenues of 27 defense companies (excluding Russia) totaled $133B in 2023, up 0.2% Y/Y. This is because the companies were mostly working on older contracts and the data does not reflect new orders.

Arms revenues for the two Russian companies in the top 100 list increased 40% to around $25.5B in 2023, and totaled $13.6B for three Israeli companies.

In Asia and Oceania, 23 companies' arms revenues were $136B, up 5.7% Y/Y. This growth was led by South Korean and Japanese companies, reflecting military build-ups in the region amid heightened threat perceptions.

Arms revenues for nine Chinese companies reached $103B in 2023, up 0.7% Y/Y - the smallest increase since 2019 due to the economic slowdown.

data.title: China factory activity grows for 2nd month; services PMI unexpectedly falls in November
data.description:
• China's official NBS Manufacturing PMI rose to 50.3 in November 2024 from 50.1 in October, aligning with market consensus and marking the highest reading since April, marking the second straight month of increase in factory activity following a series of support measures from Beijing since late September.
  • China's official NBS Non-Manufacturing PMI stood at 50.0 in November 2024, below market expectations of 50.2 which was also October's figure.
  • China's NBS Composite PMI Output Index remained at 50.8 in November 2024, holding steady for the second straight month and remaining at its highest level since May.
  • The Shanghai Composite rose 0.8% to above 3,350, while the Shenzhen Component climbed 1.2% to 10,740 on Monday, extending gains from last week as investors reacted positively to strong manufacturing data in China.

    • ETFs: (FXI), (KWEB), (CQQQ), (MCHI), (ASHR), (YINN), (TDF), (CHIQ), (GXC), (EWH), (KBA), (YANG), (CXSE), (CAF), (CWEB), (PGJ), (KURE).

    • Currency: (CNY:USD)

    • More on China

      • China's manufacturing PMI expands for second consecutive month in November to 51.5
      • PBoC holds MLF rate steady for second month
      • PBOC keeps key lending rates unchanged in November fixing, as expected
      • China's retail turnover rise to eight-month high in Oct; jobless rate fell to four-month low
      • China's CPI inflation rise less than expected in Oct, PPI continues to fall for 25th consecutive month

data.title: Keysight proposes to divest Spirent’s ethernet and network security business lines
data.description:
• Spirent Communications (OTCPK:SPMYY) and Keysight Technologies (NYSE:KEYS) on Monday said that they had reached an agreement on the terms and conditions of an all-cash acquisition of the entire issued and to be issued ordinary share capital of Spirent.
  • Furthermore, the companies said, a process was underway to divest Spirent’s (OTCPK:SPMYY) high-speed ethernet and network security business lines.

  • Keysight agreed to acquire Spirent for 201.5 pence per share in March, which includes a 2.5 pence special dividend, and gives Spirent an equity valuation of £1.16B (about$1.5B).

  • Keysight Monday confirmed that the French Ministry for the Economy, Finance, and Industry has granted a conditional authorization of the acquisition.
  • In addition, Keysight said that the deal has been approved by the German Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Climate Action and by the UK’s Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster under the National Security and Investment Act 2021.
  • The precise timing for closing of the acquisition remains subject to the pending regulatory clearances. The parties continue to keep the timetable under close review and will provide any updates as required.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Japan Manufacturing PMI declines to 49.0 in November, confirmed at 8-month low
data.description:
The au Jibun Bank Japan Manufacturing PMI declined to 49.0 in November 2024, marking the lowest figure since March, after a final 49.2 in October, matching the preliminary estimates, marking the fifth consecutive month of contraction in factory activity, as output shrank the most since April amid a sustained reduction in new orders, with subdued demand from both domestic and international markets.

"The performance of the Japanese manufacturing sector was downbeat in November. Meanwhile, capacity pressures remained subdued, as firms continued to work through outstanding business at a sharp rate in the absence of a meaningful rise in demand. This demand weakness also hindered manufacturers’ hiring activity, as firms signalled lower employment levels for the first time in nine months," said Usamah Bhatti at S&P Global Market Intelligence.

"Looking ahead, firms were optimistic that this period would pass over the coming year and new product launches would stimulate sales, whilst coinciding with a domestic and global economic recovery. The overall degree of confidence strengthened to the highest since August," he noted.

Meanwhile, separate data revealed that Japanese firms increased capital expenditure in the third quarter, suggesting resilience in corporate confidence and fueling expectations that the Bank of Japan may soon raise interest rates.

The Nikkei 225 Index dipped 0.4%, approaching 38,000, while the broader Topix Index rose 0.35% to 2,688 in mixed trading on Monday, the Japanese yen weakened past 150.5 per dollar on Monday, reversing some of last week's gains as markets remained divided on the timing of future Bank of Japan interest rate hikes.

More on Japan

data.title: Australia's retail sales rise more than expected in October by 0.6% M/M
data.description:
• Retail sales in Australia increased by 0.6% M/M in October 2024, surpassing market expectations of a 0.3% gain and accelerating from a modest 0.1% rise in September, which supported views that rate cuts in Australia are still some months away.
  • According to Robert Ewing, ABS Head of Business Statistics, "After a steady result last month, retailers reported stronger sales activity in October ahead of the Black Friday sales. The unusually strong October was driven by retailers offering early discounts, especially on discretionary items."
  • Geographically, sales rose in all states except for Northern Territory.
  • Separate data showed, Corporate profits in Australia unexpectedly shrank by 4.6% q/q in Q3 of 2024, missing market estimates of a 0.8% growth and after an upwardly revised 6.8% fall in Q2, marking the third straight month of contraction.

  • The S&P/ASX 200 Index gained 0.3% on Monday, rising above 8,460 as it moved closer to new record highs, with nearly all sectors contributing to the rally. The Australian dollar fell below $0.65 on Monday, reversing a three-day advance.

  • ETFs: (EWA), (FXA), (FLAU).

  • More on Australia

    • RBA Minutes: Discussed scenarios that could warrant rate cuts; retains restrictive monetary policy
    • Australia's trade surplus hits 4-month high despite slight declines in both exports and imports
    • Australia's monthly CPI indicator fell to three-year low of 2.7% in August
    • Reserve Bank of Australia holds cash rate steady for seventh consecutive meeting
    • Australia’s economy grows less than expected at 0.2% in Q2, misses market expectations

data.title: MINISO GAAP EPADS of $0.31 misses by $0.01, revenue of $644.5M misses by $31.05M
data.description:
• MINISO Group Holding press release (NYSE:MNSO): Q3 GAAP EPADS of $0.31 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $644.5M (+19.3% Y/Y) misses by $17.91M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA increased 12.4% year over year to $162.4 million.
  • Total number of stores on group level was 7,420 as of September 30, 2024, an increase of 859 net new stores in the first nine months of 2024.
  • Number of MINISO stores was 7,186 as of September 30, 2024, an increase of 773 net new stores in the first nine months of 2024.
  • Number of MINISO stores in mainland China was 4,250 as of September 30, 2024, a net increase of 324 in the first nine months of 2024, compared to 3,926 as of December 31, 2023.
  • Number of MINISO stores in overseas markets was 2,936 as of September 30, 2024, a net increase of 449 in the first nine months of 2024, compared to 2,487 as of December 31, 2023.
  • Number of TOP TOY stores was 234 as of September 30, 2024, with a record opening of 86 net new stores in the first nine months of 2024.

data.title: China's manufacturing PMI expands for second consecutive month in November to 51.5
data.description:
• The Caixin China General Manufacturing PMI rose to 51.5 in November 2024 from 50.3 in October, surpassing market estimates of 50.5 and marking the second straight month of increase, driven by the strongest growth in foreign orders since February 2023 and a renewed rise in exports.
  • A private survey released on Monday confirmed this expansion, bolstered by a series of stimulus measures from Beijing.
  • Investors are now looking ahead to key political meetings in China later this month for potential policy announcements.
  • Finally, confidence notched an 8-month peak on hopes of better economic conditions and supportive government policies.

  • The Shanghai Composite rose 0.8% to above 3,350, while the Shenzhen Component climbed 1.2% to 10,740 on Monday, extending gains from last week as investors reacted positively to strong manufacturing data in China.

  • ETFs: (FXI), (KWEB), (CQQQ), (MCHI), (ASHR), (YINN), (TDF), (CHIQ), (GXC), (EWH), (KBA), (YANG), (CXSE), (CAF), (CWEB), (PGJ), (KURE).

  • Currency: (CNY:USD)

  • More on China

    • PBoC holds MLF rate steady for second month
    • PBOC keeps key lending rates unchanged in November fixing, as expected
    • China's retail turnover rise to eight-month high in Oct; jobless rate fell to four-month low
    • China's CPI inflation rise less than expected in Oct, PPI continues to fall for 25th consecutive month

data.title: Northern Star to buy Australian peer De Grey Mining in A$5B deal
data.description:
Northern Star Resources (OTCPK:NESRF) said early Monday it agreed to acquire Australian gold mining peer De Grey Mining (OTCPK:DGMLF) in an all-stock deal valued at ~A$5B (~US$3.26B).

The key asset in the deal De Grey's (OTCPK:DGMLF) Hemi gold development, a Tier 1, low-cost asset in Western Australia's Pilbara region, which is expected to ramp up toward the end of the decade.

Hemi is set to produce 530K oz/year on average in the first 10 years of production, making it one of Australia's top five gold mines.

The project is still subject to a final investment decision, pending government environmental approvals, but Northern Star (OTCPK:NESRF) said it hopes to see first production by mid-2026.

Under the deal terms, De Grey (OTCPK:DGMLF) shareholders will receive 0.119 new Northern Star (OTCPK:NESRF) shares for each share held, implying an offer price of A$2.08/share, a 36.8% premium on De Grey's Friday share close of A$1.52/share.

Northern Star (OTCPK:NESRF) said its shareholders would own ~80% of the combined group, and De Grey (OTCPK:DGMLF) shareholders would hold the rest.

"Northern Star's assets are all aging, becoming more capital-intensive and facing declining grades," Citi analyst Kate McCutcheon said. "While the timing may come as a surprise to the market given recent comments around M&A... we think in the fullness of time, Northern Star can demonstrate its exploration pedigree to unlock value."
